Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Special Interrogatories (Set Two)
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ATLASSIAN US, INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Special Interrogatories (Set Two).

Defendant's motion to compel Plaintiff's responses to its requests for production of documents, set two, and special interrogatories, set two, is granted. Plaintiff has failed to timely respond to the discovery and has waived objections. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.300, subd. (a); id., sec. 2031.300, subd. (a).) Plaintiff is ordered to provide complete, verified, objection-free responses by February 23, 2026.

Plaintiff has filed an opposition that raises his self-represented status. There is no stay of discovery while a party is self-represented, and the law applies equally to self-represented litigants. Plaintiff also raises objections to the discovery requests. By failing to timely respond, he has waived objections and must provide substantially compliant responses in order to file a motion with the court seeking relief from waiver. A party who files a lawsuit takes on an obligation to provide information to the other side about his claims upon a proper discovery request

Sanctions are mandatory against a party who unsuccessfully opposes a discovery motion unless the court finds that the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.290, subd. (c); id., sec. 2031.300, subd. (c).) The court does not find substantial justification but imposes a nominal sanction of $50, which Plaintiff shall pay to Defendant by March 9, 2026. The court cautions Plaintiff that further violations of his discovery obligations could lead to further sanctions, including increased monetary sanctions or evidentiary, issue, or terminating sanctions.

Defendant shall provide notice of entry of the order to Plaintiff.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Special Interrogatories (Set One)
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SARKIS GEGAMYAN'S Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Special Interrogatories (Set One).

Plaintiff's motion to compel defendant's responses to discovery is denied. The motion is untimely, because it was filed and served fewer than 16 court days in advance of the hearing date (plus additional time depending on the method of service). (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1005, subd. (b).) In addition, a party who receives a discovery request has 30 days to respond (plus additional time depending on the method of service). (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.260, subd. (a).) Defendant's responses are not yet due pursuant to the Code. (Carradero Decl., para. 2 [electronic service on Jan. 21, 2026].)

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
